name: Las Fallas
traditions: Building massive satirical papier-mâché monuments (fallas); La Cremà (burning of all fallas); mascleta (daily firecrackers); flower offering to Virgin Mary.
historicalBackground: Las Fallas originated from carpenters burning wooden forms (parots) on St. Joseph's Day. Over centuries, it evolved into an elaborate artistic tradition.
description: Valencia's spectacular fire festival featuring towering artistic monuments that are paraded, judged, and then set ablaze in a dramatic finale.
